info.name: Discovery Hub API
info.description:
# Discovery Hub API

**Discovery Hub** is a full-featured church management backend built for modern, growing congregations. It handles everything from Sunday check-ins and live service programme running to tithe statements, first-timer follow-up pipelines, finance requests, and children's church pickup codes — all through a single, secure REST API.

Deployable anywhere. Configurable for any congregation — church name, address, logo, currency, and timezone are all driven by environment variables.

Base URL: `http://localhost:3000/v1`

---

## Actor Types

| Actor | How they access | Token variable |
|-------|----------------|---------------|
| **Member** | Mobile app — regular church member | `{{accessToken}}` |
| **Worker** | Mobile app — serves in a department (role: WORKER) | `{{accessToken}}` |
| **Admin** | Web portal — separate Admin entity linked to a member | `{{admin_token}}` |

A member becomes a Worker via `POST /members/:id/promote`. Admin access is granted separately via `POST /admin/users`.

---

## Authentication Flow

### Members and Workers
1. **Sign Up** — `POST /auth/signup` (no password in body; server generates one and emails it)
2. **Login** — `POST /auth/login` with `email`, `password`, and `deviceId`
   - Auto-captures `{{accessToken}}` and `{{refreshToken}}` via the test script
   - If `requires_password_change: true`, call `POST /auth/change-password` before anything else
3. **Refresh** — `POST /auth/refresh` (pass `{{refreshToken}}` as Bearer header) when access token expires
4. **Logout** — `POST /auth/logout` to invalidate the session

### Admin
1. **Admin Portal Login** — `POST /auth/admin-login` with `email` and `password`
   - Returns tokens; capture `admin_token` manually or add a test script
   - No device lock — web-first route

---

## Token Lifecycle

- **Access token** expires in **1 hour** (`expires_in: 3600`)
- **Refresh token** is long-lived; use it once to get a new pair
- Logging out invalidates both tokens server-side (Redis blocklist)

---

## Device Binding

The first login from a device registers `deviceId` against the member record. All subsequent logins from a **different** `deviceId` return **403**. To switch devices:
- Self-service: `POST /auth/device-reset/request` → OTP email → `POST /auth/device-reset/verify`
- Admin override: `DELETE /members/:id/device` (clears lock, member may log in from any device)

---

## Collection Variables

| Variable | Stores |
|----------|--------|
| `baseUrl` | `http://localhost:3000/v1` |
| `accessToken` | Member/Worker JWT (auto-set by Login test script) |
| `refreshToken` | Refresh token (auto-set by Login test script) |
| `memberId` | Primary member UUID under test |
| `memberId2` | Secondary member UUID (e.g. bulk operations) |
| `departmentId` | Department UUID |
| `eventId` | Event UUID |
| `slotId` | ServiceSlot UUID |
| `configId` | EventConfig UUID |
| `classId` | Church class UUID |
| `enrollmentId` | Enrollment UUID |
| `leaveId` | Leave request UUID |
| `venueId` | Venue UUID (auto-set by Create Venue test script) |
| `ssClassId` | Sunday School class UUID |
| `ssSessionId` | Sunday School session UUID |
| `ageGroupId` | Children Church age group UUID |
| `classGroupId` | Children Church class group UUID |
| `childId` | Child profile UUID |
| `guardianId` | Guardian UUID |
| `checkinId` | Children Church check-in UUID (auto-set by Check In Child) |
| `pickupCode` | 6-char pickup code (auto-set by Check In Child) |
| `reminderId` | Event reminder UUID (auto-set by Create Reminder) |
| `wishRecipientId` | Target member UUID for birthday wishes |
| `adminRoleId` | Admin role UUID |
| `adminUserId` | Admin user record UUID |
| `admin_token` | Admin JWT (set manually after admin login) |

---

## Common Patterns

**Pagination** — pass `?page=1&limit=20` on list endpoints. Response shape:
```json
{ "data": [...], "page": 1, "limit": 20, "totalCount": 150, "totalPages": 8 }
```

**Error shape:**
```json
{ "statusCode": 400, "message": "Descriptive error message" }
```

**Enum values** — call `GET /enums` (mobile) or `GET /admin/enums` (portal) on app launch to populate all dropdowns without hardcoding.

**UUIDs** — all IDs are UUID v4, e.g. `a3f2e1b4-9c7d-4e5f-8a6b-1c2d3e4f5a6b`

---

## Quick-Start (Mobile App)

1. `POST /auth/signup` — register a new member (password arrives by email)
2. `POST /auth/login` — log in; `accessToken` and `refreshToken` are auto-saved
3. `GET /auth/me` — fetch profile; save the `id` as `{{memberId}}`
4. `GET /enums` — load all dropdown values
5. `GET /events?upcoming=true` — list upcoming services
6. `POST /attendances/checkin` with `serviceSlotId` — check in to a service

item.name: Auth
item.description:
Handles all authentication flows for both mobile app users (members/workers) and the admin web portal.

**Access:** Public (no token) for sign-up, login, forgot-password, reset-password. JWT required for logout, change-password, and device-reset-verify.

**Typical flow:**
1. Sign Up → password emailed to member
2. Login → captures `{{accessToken}}` and `{{refreshToken}}`
3. If `requires_password_change: true` → Change Password immediately
4. When token expires → Refresh Token
5. Switching devices → Request Device Reset → Verify Device Reset

**Key constraints:**
- One device per member. First `deviceId` sent at login is locked in. Subsequent logins from a different device return 403.
- OTP codes (forgot-password, device-reset) are rate-limited and single-use.
- Admin login (`/auth/admin-login`) has no device lock — web-only route.

request.description: Register a new member account. Do NOT include a password — the server generates a secure temporary password and emails it to the provided address. The account will have changedPassword=false, requiring a password change on first login.

Mobile app login (members & workers). Requires deviceId — the first login from a device registers it; subsequent logins must match the registered device or receive 403. Returns requires_password_change: true when a forced password change is pending.
Admin portal login (web). Validates credentials AND verifies the caller has an active Admin record. Returns 403 if the member has no admin access. No device lock — this route is web-first.

response.name: 403 No Admin Access
response.body: { "statusCode": 403, "message": "This account does not have admin access" }

request.description: Exchange a refresh token for a new access token. Pass the refresh token in the Authorization header.
Invalidate current session. Returns 204 No Content.
Returns own member profile including isHod: boolean (true if the member is a Head of Department). Clients should call this once on load to determine HOD status.

Change password for the authenticated user. Requires the current password. `newPassword` and `confirmPassword` must match. On success, `changedPassword` is set to `true` on the member record. Auth: JwtAuthGuard.

response.name: 400 Passwords do not match
response.body: { "statusCode": 400, "message": "New password and confirm password do not match" }

request.description: Request a 6-digit OTP reset code. Rate-limited (default 3 attempts/hr). Always returns the same message regardless of whether the email exists, to avoid account enumeration.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body: { "message": "If this email is registered, a reset code has been sent." }

response.name: 429 Rate Limited
response.body: { "statusCode": 429, "message": "Too many reset attempts. Please try again later." }

request.description: Verify OTP and set a new password. Marks the OTP as used, invalidates the current session, and sends a confirmation email. The user must log in fresh after this.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body: { "message": "Password reset successfully. Please log in with your new password." }

request.description: Self-service device reset step 1. Rate-limited (default 3 per 24 h per email). Sends a 6-digit OTP to the registered email address. The newDeviceId submitted here is locked in — the OTP cannot be used to register a different device.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body: { "message": "Device reset OTP sent to your registered email." }

response.name: 429 Rate Limited
response.body: { "statusCode": 429, "message": "Too many device reset requests. Try again in 24 hours." }

request.description: Self-service device reset step 2. Verifies the OTP, updates member.deviceId to the newDeviceId submitted in the request step, invalidates all active sessions, and sends a confirmation email. Member must log in fresh from the new device.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body: { "message": "Device reset successful. Please log in from your new device." }

item.name: Members
item.description:
Manage the Member entity — the root record for every person in the system.

**Access:** Admin-only for most write operations. Any authenticated member can read their own profile (`GET /members/me`).

**Key concepts:**
- `status`: `ACTIVE` (default) or `INACTIVE`. Use `PATCH /:id/status` to deactivate. **Member deletion is intentionally not exposed.**
- `role`: `MEMBER` (default) or `WORKER`. Promote via `POST /:id/promote` (creates a WorkerProfile); demote via `POST /:id/revoke-worker`.
- Workers have a `workerProfile` with `departmentId`, `profession`, `status`, and optional `secondaryDepartmentId`.
- Device lock: each member may only log in from one registered device. Admins can clear the lock via `DELETE /:id/device`.

**Typical flow:**
1. `POST /auth/signup` creates the member
2. Admin calls `GET /members` to find and review new registrations
3. `POST /:id/promote` to make the member a worker
4. `PATCH /:id/worker-profile` to update department or training flags
5. `PATCH /:id/status` to deactivate if the member leaves

query.description: Case-insensitive match on firstname, lastname, email or phone

request.description: Admin only (MEMBERS_READ). Returns paginated list of all members with role=WORKER. Each entry includes workerProfile with department. Optional `status` filter: ACTIVE | INACTIVE.

query.description: Filter by worker status: ACTIVE | INACTIVE (optional)

request.description: Any authenticated member (JwtAuthGuard). Returns the caller's own member profile including workerProfile and department relations. Used by the mobile app on launch to populate the logged-in user's profile.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "id": "a3f2e1b4-9c7d-4e5f-8a6b-1c2d3e4f5a6b",
  "firstname": "John",
  "lastname": "Doe",
  "email": "john.doe@example.com",
  "phoneNumber": "+2348012345678",
  "role": "WORKER",
  "status": "ACTIVE",
  "gender": "MALE",
  "maritalStatus": "MARRIED",
  "birthDay": 15,
  "birthMonth": 5,
  "birthYear": 1990,
  "changedPassword": true,
  "workerProfile": {
    "id": "c5d4e3f2-7a6b-4c5d-8e9f-0a1b2c3d4e5f",
    "status": "ACTIVE",
    "profession": "Software Engineer",
    "yearJoinedWorkforce": "2022",
    "completedSOD": true,
    "completedBibleCollege": false,
    "department": { "id": "d6e5f4a3-8b7c-4d6e-9f0a-1b2c3d4e5f6a", "name": "Sunday School", "key": "SUNDAY_SCHOOL" },
    "secondaryDepartment": null
  }
}

request.description: Admin or the member themselves. Returns full member profile including workerProfile and department if applicable.
All fields are optional. Only provided fields are updated.
Creates a WorkerProfile for the member, assigns them to a department and sets role to WORKER. departmentId is required; profession and yearJoinedWorkforce are optional.
Removes the WorkerProfile and resets the member's role back to MEMBER. Returns 204 No Content.

Update a worker's profile fields. All fields are optional. Pass `"secondaryDepartmentId": null` to remove the secondary department assignment. Omitting `secondaryDepartmentId` entirely leaves the current value unchanged. ADMIN only.
Activate or deactivate a member. status: ACTIVE | INACTIVE. Returns 204.
Generates a new random password and emails it to the member. The member will need to change it on next login.

Admin (MEMBERS_WRITE): Remove the registered deviceId for a member and invalidate all active sessions. This resets the one-device-per-member lock, allowing the member to log in from any device. Responds with 204 No Content.

item.name: Admin
item.description:
Manages admin portal users (roles + access grants) and audit logs. Admin access is separate from the member/worker role system — an `Admin` entity is linked to a Member record and carries a permission set.

**Access:** All endpoints require AdminGuard with specific permissions. The super-admin role has all permissions by default.

**Key concepts:**
- `AdminRole` — a named template of permissions (e.g. "FinanceTeam" with FINANCE_READ + FINANCE_WRITE). Create roles first.
- `Admin User` — grants an existing member access to the portal by linking them to an AdminRole. The member logs in at `POST /auth/admin-login`.
- Permissions gate individual actions (e.g. MEMBERS_WRITE, ATTENDANCE_READ). Call `GET /admin/enums` to list all valid permission strings.
- Audit logs capture every admin action with actor, target, action type, and timestamp.

**Typical flow:**
1. Create an AdminRole with the desired permissions
2. Grant a member admin access (`POST /admin/users`) — sends a welcome email
3. Member logs in at `POST /auth/admin-login`
4. Review admin actions via `GET /admin/audit-logs`

item.name: Admin Roles
item.description: Admin role templates that bundle permissions. Create a role, then grant it to members via Admin Users.

request.description: AdminGuard (ADMIN_READ). Returns all admin role templates with their permission arrays.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: AdminGuard (ADMIN_WRITE). Creates a new admin role template. permissions is an array of permission strings — call GET /admin/enums to get valid values. name must be unique.

response.name: 409 Duplicate name
response.body: { "statusCode": 409, "message": "An admin role with this name already exists" }

item.name: Admin Users
item.description: Grant and revoke admin portal access for existing members. Requires ADMIN_WRITE to create/update/revoke.

request.description: AdminGuard (ADMIN_READ). Returns all admin user records with their linked member and role.
Grants admin portal access to an existing member. Requires ADMIN_WRITE permission. Sends a welcome-admin email to the member with their email address and the portal login URL.
Soft-revokes admin access (sets isActive=false). Requires ADMIN_WRITE permission. Sends an account-deactivated notification email to the affected member.

item.name: Audit Logs
item.description: Immutable log of every significant admin action (role grants, permission changes, member status changes). Admin-only. Supports pagination and optional `actorId` / `action` filters.

request.description: Admin (AUDIT_READ): Paginated audit log. Supports optional filters: action, actorId, targetId, dateFrom, dateTo. Returns paginated AuditLog records with the actor relation loaded (actor name/email included).

item.name: Attendance
item.description:
Tracks member and worker attendance at church services. One attendance record per member per event (not per slot).

**Access:**
- Check-in: any authenticated member or worker (`JwtAuthGuard`)
- My history: any authenticated user
- Admin views (all history, slot summary, leaderboard): AdminGuard with ATTENDANCE_READ
- Department view: WORKER (department HOD/D-HOD)
- Correction: AdminGuard with ATTENDANCE_WRITE

**Key concepts:**
- Checking in to **any slot** of an event covers the whole event. You cannot check in twice to the same event.
- **Workers must include GPS coordinates** (`location.latitude`, `location.longitude`). Distance from venue is validated against `EventConfig.allowedDistanceInMeters`.
- Members are always marked `PRESENT`. Workers are `PRESENT` or `LATE` depending on `EventConfig.workerLateOffsetSeconds` relative to the slot start time.
- **Online attendance**: workers who miss in-person can confirm `ATTENDED_ONLINE` via `POST /attendances/online-confirm` within a configurable window after service.
- Status values: `PRESENT` | `LATE` | `ABSENT` | `ON_LEAVE` | `ATTENDED_ONLINE`

**Typical flow:**
1. Admin creates an Event with ServiceSlots (each with a ConfigId)
2. On service day, member opens the app and calls `POST /attendances/checkin` with the `serviceSlotId`
3. Worker includes `location` in the same call
4. Admin reviews via `GET /attendances/history` or `GET /attendances/summary/slot/:slotId`
5. Admin corrects errors via `PATCH /attendances/:id/correct`

request.description: Any authenticated user (MEMBER or WORKER) can check in. Workers MUST include location coordinates. Members may omit location. Location distance is validated if ENFORCE_DISTANCE_CHECK=true. One record per member per event — checking in to any slot of an event covers the whole event. Members are always PRESENT; workers are PRESENT or LATE depending on workerLateOffsetSeconds. Duplicate check-ins return 409 (enforced at DB level via unique constraint on (member, event)).

response.name: 409 Already checked in
response.body: { "statusCode": 409, "message": "You have already checked in for this event." }

response.name: 400 Worker missing location
response.body: { "statusCode": 400, "message": "Workers must provide their location to check in." }

request.description: Returns the authenticated user's own attendance records. Available to all roles.

query.description: ILIKE search on member firstname, lastname, or email (optional)

request.description: WORKER only (department lead). Returns attendance for their department for a given slot.
Returns PRESENT/LATE/ABSENT/ON_LEAVE counts for a single service slot.
Returns top workers ranked by present+late count within the time window.
Returns attendance records for the caller's department for a specific event. Includes all service slots within that event. WORKER only (resolves the caller's department from their profile).
Admin correction of a specific attendance record. Requires ATTENDANCE_WRITE permission. Body: { status: PRESENT | LATE | ABSENT | ON_LEAVE }.

Confirms that the authenticated member attended the event online. Valid only while the online window is open (ONLINE_CHECKIN_WINDOW_HOURS after notification was sent). Updates the ABSENT record to ATTENDED_ONLINE.

response.name: 404 No record
response.body: { "statusCode": 404, "message": "No attendance record found for this event" }

item.name: Events
item.description:
Manages church events (Sunday services, midweek services, etc.) and their service slots.

**Access:** Admin-only for create/update/delete. Any authenticated user can list and get events.

**Key concepts:**
- Each event has one or more `serviceSlots`, each defining a time window and referencing an `EventConfig`.
- **Recurring events** (`isRecurring: true`) generate one Event record per weekly occurrence up to `recurrenceEndDate` (max 1 year). All occurrences share the same slot template.
- **Slot times** must fall within the event date range. Slots are auto-sorted and validated for no overlap.
- To delete an entire recurring series, use `DELETE /events/recurring/:recurringEventId` — this removes all future occurrences.
- **You must create an EventConfig and Venue before creating events** (slots reference `configId` and optionally `venueOverrideId`).

**Typical flow:**
1. Create a Venue (`POST /venues`)
2. Create an EventConfig (`POST /event-config`) referencing the venue
3. Create the Event (`POST /events`) with slots referencing the configId
4. Reference slot UUIDs in attendance check-in and reminders

request.description: Create a single non-recurring event. endDate defaults to eventDate if omitted. Slot times must fall within [eventDate 00:00, endDate 23:59]. Slots are auto-sorted ASC and validated for no overlap.

response.name: 201 Created

request.description: Minimal single-event example — one slot with a configId. At least one serviceSlot is required; slots without a configId will fail at check-in time.

Creates one event record per recurrence date up to recurrenceEndDate (max 1 year ahead). serviceSlots are required (at least one) and must include a configId. The same slot template is stamped on every occurrence — all share the same config. Delete future occurrences in bulk via DELETE /events/recurring/:recurringEventId.

Updates event metadata. When `serviceSlots` is provided (min 1 entry), ALL existing slots are replaced with the new ones — include `configId` on each slot to preserve config linkage. Omit `serviceSlots` entirely to leave existing slots unchanged. ADMIN only.
Any authenticated user. Returns the event with its service slots and config info.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "id": "e5f6a7b8-c9d0-1e2f-3a4b-5c6d7e8f9a0b",
  "name": "Sunday Service",
  "description": "Regular Sunday morning service",
  "eventDate": "2026-06-15",
  "endDate": "2026-06-15",
  "isRecurring": false,
  "onlineAttendanceEnabled": true,
  "serviceSlots": [
    {
      "id": "f6a7b8c9-d0e1-2f3a-4b5c-6d7e8f9a0b1c",
      "name": "First Service",
      "startTime": "2026-06-15T07:00:00.000Z",
      "endTime": "2026-06-15T09:00:00.000Z",
      "config": { "id": "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7a8b-9c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6d", "name": "Default Sunday Config", "allowedDistanceInMeters": 150 },
      "venueOverride": null
    }
  ],
  "createdAt": "2026-06-10T08:00:00.000Z",
  "updatedAt": "2026-06-10T08:00:00.000Z"
}

request.description: Any authenticated user. Returns paginated events, optionally filtered by date range. Use `?upcoming=true` to get only future events (eventDate >= today).

query.description: YYYY-MM-DD — filter events on or after this date
YYYY-MM-DD — filter events on or before this date
true — return only future events (eventDate >= today)

request.description: ADMIN only. Cannot delete past events.

variable.description: The shared recurringEventId UUID from the event series

request.description: ADMIN only. Deletes all future events in a recurring series (today and after).

item.name: Event Config
item.description:
Reusable check-in configuration templates attached to service slots. Defines when check-in opens/closes and what distance from venue is acceptable.

**Access:** Admin-only for all operations.

**Key concepts:**
- All time offsets are in **seconds relative to the slot's startTime**. Negative values mean "before the slot starts".
- `workerCheckinStartOffsetSeconds` — how early workers may check in (e.g. -5400 = 90 min before)
- `workerLateOffsetSeconds` — workers arriving after this offset are marked LATE (e.g. 0 = late if after slot start)
- `memberCheckinStartOffsetSeconds` — how early members may check in (e.g. -1800 = 30 min before)
- `checkinStopOffsetSeconds` — when check-in closes for everyone (e.g. 3600 = 1 hour after slot start)
- `allowedDistanceInMeters` — max GPS distance for worker check-in (default 150m)
- `defaultVenueId` — required; the venue whose coordinates are used for distance checks
- Constraint: `workerLateOffset > workerCheckinStartOffset` and `checkinStopOffset > workerLateOffset`
- A config cannot be deleted while service slots still reference it.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Default Sunday Config",
  "description": "Standard check-in config for Sunday services",
  "defaultVenueId": "{{venueId}}",
  "workerCheckinStartOffsetSeconds": -5400,
  "workerLateOffsetSeconds": 0,
  "memberCheckinStartOffsetSeconds": -1800,
  "checkinStopOffsetSeconds": 3600,
  "allowedDistanceInMeters": 150
}

request.description: Offsets are in seconds relative to slot startTime. Negative = before slot starts. Constraint: workerLateOffset > workerCheckinStartOffset and checkinStopOffset > workerLateOffset. defaultVenueId is required — create a Venue first and reference it here.
Admin only. Returns the config with all offset values and the default venue.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "id": "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7a8b-9c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6d",
  "name": "Default Sunday Config",
  "description": "Standard check-in config for Sunday services",
  "workerCheckinStartOffsetSeconds": -5400,
  "workerLateOffsetSeconds": 0,
  "memberCheckinStartOffsetSeconds": -1800,
  "checkinStopOffsetSeconds": 3600,
  "allowedDistanceInMeters": 150,
  "defaultVenue": {
    "id": "b2c3d4e5-f6a7-8b9c-0d1e-2f3a4b5c6d7e",
    "name": "Main Auditorium",
    "latitude": 6.5244,
    "longitude": 3.3792
  },
  "createdAt": "2026-01-01T00:00:00.000Z"
}

request.description: Fails if any service slot is still assigned to this config.

item.name: Departments
item.description:
Church departments (Ushering, Choir, Sunday School, etc.) that workers are assigned to.

**Access:** Admin-only for create/update/delete/assign-lead. Any authenticated user can list departments and read department details. Department leads (HOD/D-HOD) can view their own department summary.

**Key concepts:**
- Each department has an optional `key` (DepartmentKeyEnum) that gates module access. Example: a department with `key: SUNDAY_SCHOOL` grants workers access to the Sunday School module.
- Multiple departments may share the same key. Call `GET /departments/keys` for all valid values.
- Workers have a **primary** department and an optional **secondary** department (via WorkerProfile).
- Department leads (`type: head` or `type: assistant`) are assigned from workers whose primary department matches. They unlock the department summary and can view department-level attendance and leave.

**Typical flow:**
1. Create department (`POST /departments`) with optional `key`
2. Promote members to workers and assign them to this department
3. Assign a HOD via `POST /departments/assign-lead` (type: head)
4. HOD can view `GET /departments/my/summary` for their team stats

request.description: Returns the list of all valid DepartmentKeyEnum values. Use this to populate key-selection dropdowns when creating or updating a department. No auth required beyond a valid JWT.
Public (JWT only). Returns paginated list of departments.
Any authenticated user. Returns department with its leads (HOD and D-HOD) if assigned.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "id": "d6e5f4a3-8b7c-4d6e-9f0a-1b2c3d4e5f6a",
  "name": "Sunday School Department",
  "description": "Oversees all Sunday School classes and sessions",
  "key": "SUNDAY_SCHOOL",
  "head": { "id": "c5d4e3f2-7a6b-4c5d-8e9f-0a1b2c3d4e5f", "member": { "firstname": "Jane", "lastname": "Smith" } },
  "assistant": null,
  "createdAt": "2025-06-01T00:00:00.000Z"
}

request.description: ADMIN only. The optional `key` field links this department to a module-access category (DepartmentKeyEnum). Multiple departments may share the same key. Omit `key` (or pass null) if the department does not gate any module. Example keys: SUNDAY_SCHOOL, CHILDREN_CHURCH, MEDIA.
ADMIN only. All fields are optional. The `key` field can be updated to change module access. Set to null to remove module gating.
ADMIN only (DEPARTMENTS_WRITE). Returns 400 if the department has workers currently assigned to it as their primary or secondary department.
Assigns a worker as department head or assistant. type: 'head' | 'assistant'. The worker must have this department as their primary department to be assigned as HOD.

response.name: 404 Not Found

request.description: Paginated list of all workers whose primary department is this one. Each worker entry includes a `leadRole` field ("head" | "assistant" | null). ADMIN only.

Returns aggregated stats for the department the caller leads (HOD or D-HOD): total/active/inactive worker counts, 30-day attendance %, and workers currently on approved or pending leave. WORKER (HOD/D-HOD) only — throws 403 if caller is not a department lead.

item.name: Leave
item.description:
Leave request management for workers who will be absent during a service period.

**Access:**
- Request Leave, Delete Leave, My History, Department Leave: WORKER only
- Action Leave (approve/reject), All History: Admin only (LEAVE_WRITE / LEAVE_READ)

**Key concepts:**
- Workers create a PENDING leave request with a date range. Admin approves or rejects.
- Approved leave changes the worker's attendance status from ABSENT to ON_LEAVE for events during that period.
- Workers can only delete their own PENDING requests (not approved or rejected ones).
- Department leads can view leave requests from their department members via `GET /leave/department`.
- Status flow: `PENDING` → `APPROVED` or `REJECTED`

**Typical flow:**
1. Worker: `POST /leave` with dateFrom, dateTo, reason
2. Admin: `GET /leave/history?status=PENDING` to review pending requests
3. Admin: `PATCH /leave/:id/action` with `{ "status": "APPROVED" }` or `"REJECTED"`
4. Approved leave is automatically applied to attendance for the covered dates

item.name: Announcements
item.description:
In-app announcement system for church communications. Supports targeted delivery by audience.

**Access:** Admin-only for create/update/delete. Any authenticated user can view their feed.

**Audience types:**
- `ALL` — visible to all members and workers
- `WORKERS_ONLY` — visible only to workers
- `DEPARTMENT` — visible to workers in a specific department (`departmentId` required)
- `INDIVIDUAL` — private announcement to one member (`targetMemberId` required)

**Key concepts:**
- `publishedAt` defaults to now if omitted. `expiresAt` is optional; expired announcements are excluded from feeds.
- `GET /announcements/feed` returns the caller's relevant announcements: members see ALL, workers see ALL + WORKERS_ONLY + their department.
- `GET /announcements/all` (admin) returns all announcements regardless of expiry.

**Typical flow:**
1. Admin: `POST /announcements` with title, body, audience (and departmentId/targetMemberId if needed)
2. Member/Worker: `GET /announcements/feed` to see their personalized feed
3. Admin: `PATCH /announcements/:id` to extend expiry or update content

item.name: Tithes (Admin)
item.description:
Admin-side tithe management: bank account setup, bulk Excel upload, unmatched row resolution, dispute handling, and payment proof review.

**Access:** AdminGuard — FINANCE_READ for reads, FINANCE_WRITE for mutations.

**Key concepts:**
- `TitheAccount` — a bank account records are credited to. Create accounts first; members select them when submitting proofs.
- **Bulk upload flow:** Download template → fill rows (Email, Amount, Date, Ref, Bank) → Upload batch → Bull queue processes rows → Matched rows create TitheRecords → Unmatched rows (no member with that email) go to the unmatched queue → Disputes (duplicate record) go to disputes queue.
- `UnmatchedRow` — manually match to a member or dismiss (e.g. test entry)
- `TitheDispute` — duplicate detected; approve to create the record anyway, reject to discard
- `TithePaymentProof` — member-submitted receipts. Admin confirms (creates TitheRecord) or declines (with a reason)

**Typical flow:**
1. Create TitheAccount(s)
2. Upload batch via `POST /admin/tithes/upload`
3. Check batch status via `GET /admin/tithes/batches/:id`
4. Resolve unmatched: `GET /admin/tithes/unmatched` → `POST /:id/match` or `POST /:id/dismiss`
5. Resolve disputes: `GET /admin/tithes/disputes` → `PATCH /:id/approve` or `PATCH /:id/reject`
6. Review member-submitted proofs: `GET /admin/tithes/proofs` → `POST /:id/confirm` or `POST /:id/decline`

item.name: Webhooks

Provider webhook receivers. These endpoints have no JWT guard — they are verified by HMAC signature.

item.name: Finance (Admin)
item.description:
Admin-side management of department finance requests raised by HODs.

**Access:** AdminGuard — FINANCE_READ for list/get, FINANCE_WRITE for approve/reject/proof.

**Key concepts:**
- Finance categories group requests by expense type (e.g. Equipment, Printing, Transport). Admin creates categories first.
- HODs raise requests with an amount, recipient bank details, reason, and optional attachment.
- Admin reviews and approves or rejects (rejection requires a reason, which is emailed to the HOD).
- After approving, admin can attach a payment proof file via `PATCH /admin/finance/requests/:id/proof` to confirm disbursement.

**Status flow:** `PENDING` → `APPROVED` (admin approves) → proof attached OR `REJECTED` (with reason)

request.description: AdminGuard (FINANCE_READ). Returns all finance categories sorted by name.
AdminGuard (FINANCE_WRITE). Creates a new expense category. Name must be unique.
AdminGuard (FINANCE_WRITE). Updates an existing category.

AdminGuard (FINANCE_READ). Paginated list of all finance requests. Filters: status (PENDING/APPROVED/REJECTED), categoryId, memberId, departmentId, search (wildcard on requester name, email, or reason).
AdminGuard (FINANCE_READ). Downloads all matching finance requests as finance-requests.xlsx. Supports same filters as the list endpoint; no pagination — all matching rows are included.
AdminGuard (FINANCE_READ). Get a single finance request with full relations.
AdminGuard (FINANCE_WRITE). Approves a PENDING request. Notifies the HOD by email.
AdminGuard (FINANCE_WRITE). Rejects a PENDING request. Requires a rejectionReason. Notifies the HOD by email.
AdminGuard (FINANCE_WRITE). Uploads payment proof to Cloudinary and attaches the URL to an APPROVED request. Notifies the HOD by email.

item.name: Follow-Up (Worker)
item.description:
First-timer registration and follow-up task management for workers in the Follow-Up department.

**Access:** WORKER only. Caller must be assigned to the department with `key: FOLLOW_UP`.

**Key concepts:**
- Registering a first-timer auto-creates a `FollowUpTask` assigned round-robin to an active Follow-Up department member.
- Workers see only their **own** assigned tasks.
- Task statuses: `PENDING` | `IN_PROGRESS` | `COMPLETED` | `UNREACHABLE`
- Task outcomes: `JOINED` | `DECLINED` | `NO_ANSWER` | `PRAYED_WITH`
- `noteContent` in the update body optionally creates an attached note for audit.

**Typical flow:**
1. Worker registers first-timer at the door: `POST /follow-up/first-timers`
2. Worker views their task queue: `GET /follow-up/tasks/mine`
3. Worker calls/visits and updates: `PATCH /follow-up/tasks/:id` with status and outcome

Admin-level follow-up management: full visibility of first-timers and tasks, reassignment, bulk updates, and pastoral reporting.

**Access:** AdminGuard — FOLLOW_UP_READ for list/report, FOLLOW_UP_WRITE for mutations.

**Typical flow:**
1. `GET /admin/follow-up/first-timers` — review all registered first-timers
2. `GET /admin/follow-up/tasks?status=PENDING` — see unresolved tasks
3. `PATCH /admin/follow-up/tasks/:id/reassign` — move a task to a different Follow-Up worker

5. `GET /admin/follow-up/report` — review conversion rate, source breakdown, and per-worker performance

item.name: Service Programme
item.description:
Order-of-service programme builder with live session control. Admin creates and populates the programme; Admin-department workers run it live from the mobile app.

**Access:**
- Programme CRUD: AdminGuard
- Session control (start/advance/rewind/pause/resume/end/override): WORKER in the Admin department (`key: ADMIN`)
- Session state (read-only): public — the `sessionCode` is the credential
- Reports and analytics: AdminGuard

**Key concepts:**
- A `ServiceProgramme` is linked 1:1 to a `serviceSlot`. One programme per slot.
- Programmes have `ProgrammeSlot` items (type: `SPEAKER` | `BREAK`) with ordering, topic, assigned member, and allocatedMinutes.
- Programmes progress: `DRAFT` (editable) → `LIVE` (session running) → `COMPLETED` (session ended)
- When started, a live `ServiceSession` is created with a `sessionCode` (e.g. SVC-ABC123). Clients poll `GET /v1/service-session/:sessionCode/state` to track progress.
- `saveAsTemplate: true` auto-upserts a `ServiceProgrammeTemplate` on session end for reuse.
- PDFs: download the programme runsheet before service, or the full session report after.

**Typical flow:**
1. Admin: Create programme linked to a service slot
2. Admin: Add slots (SPEAKER/BREAK), set members and topics, reorder as needed
3. Worker (Admin dept): `POST /v1/service-session/programme/:programmeId/start` — session goes LIVE
4. Worker: `POST /v1/service-session/:sessionCode/advance` as each slot completes
5. Worker: `POST /v1/service-session/:sessionCode/end` — session COMPLETED, template saved
6. Admin: `GET /v1/service-session/:sessionCode/report` for the post-service analysis

item.name: Service Headcount
item.description:
Manual headcount recording per service slot — tracks congregation by demographic group (male adults, female adults, teenagers, children, mobile church, and custom groups).

**Access:** Admin-department workers (primary or secondary department `key: ADMIN`) or AdminGuard.

**Key concepts:**
- One headcount record per service slot. A second `POST` for the same slot returns 409.
- Corrections are allowed at any time via `PATCH /v1/service-headcount/:id` (partial update).
- `customGroups` is a flexible JSON map for additional demographic splits.
- `GET /v1/service-headcount/trends` returns weekly/monthly aggregates for admin reporting.

**Typical flow:**
1. After service: ushers count attendees per demographic group
2. Worker or admin: `POST /v1/service-headcount` with slot ID and counts
3. If recount differs: `PATCH /v1/service-headcount/:id` with corrected values and updated notes

request.description: Record attendance headcount for a service slot.
